title: DHS Shutdown Pauses 85% of OIG Audits — Including ICE Use-of-Force Reviews
excerpt: A partial government shutdown cutting DHS funding forced the DHS Office of Inspector General to pause approximately 85% of its audits, inspections, and oversight reviews — including active probes into whether ICE investigates excessive force allegations and whether detention facilities meet safety standards. ICE itself remained fully funded and continued operations throughout the shutdown.
content:
In mid-February 2026, a partial government shutdown that halted discretionary DHS funding caused the DHS Office of Inspector General to suspend the vast majority of its oversight work, creating a significant accountability gap at the precise moment ICE enforcement was at peak intensity.

  • Approximately 85% of the OIG's audits, evaluations, and inspections were paused, including seven active reviews involving ICE and CBP conduct.
  • Paused reviews included the determination of whether ICE investigates allegations of excessive force and holds personnel accountable, and unannounced inspections to assess compliance with ICE detention standards.
  • An investigation into whether CBP agents were conducting domestic U.S. operations in accordance with DHS policies and federal law was also halted — a particularly notable gap given the fatal shootings in Minneapolis.
  • Approximately 60% of the OIG's workforce was furloughed, including auditors, data scientists, and inspectors conducting the paused reviews.
  • ICE itself was unaffected by the shutdown, having received approximately $75 billion in multi-year mandatory funding through prior legislation, and continued to make arrests throughout.
  • NBC News reported that the OIG's office was managing 650 investigations, 60 audits, and 20 inspections, and receiving more than 20,000 complaints per year at the time of the pause.
"In a shutdown, agents keep working and audits stop," noted one analysis, describing the structural design as allowing enforcement to continue while accountability stops.
mediaPlaceholder: OIG statement to NBC News listing paused audits, contrasted with ICE arrest operations continuing under separate appropriation
imageAlt: DHS Shutdown Pauses 85% of OIG Audits — Including ICE Use-of-Force Reviews
